name: Candied Grapefruit
recipeInstructions:
1. SCORE GRAPEFRUIT INTO QUARTERS and carefully peel off the peels or juice the fruits, cutting halves in half again.
2. Place peels in a large pan, cover with cold water, and bring to boil. Simmer for 25 minutes, then remove peels and let them cool until manageable.
3. Gently scrape away most of the white pith just below the skin using a knife or scissors to cut cleaned peels into strips as narrow or wide as desired.
4. In a 2-quart saucepan, mix together sugar, water, and corn syrup. Bring to boil.
5. When syrup is clear, add peels, lower heat, and cook slowly until peels are translucent, about 1 hour.
6. Set a cake rack over a baking sheet. Remove peels a few at a time and spread them out on the rack to drain for about 15 minutes.
7. While draining, cover a large plate with superfine sugar. Place several pieces of peels in the sugar and turn to coat evenly.
8. When coated, set peels aside on another plate or piece of parchment paper thinly coated with sugar. If sugar becomes lumpy with syrup beads, strain it before continuing.
9. Set sugared peels aside for about 1 hour or until dried completely.
10. Carefully package the candied grapefruit pieces in a tin or plastic container and store in the cupboard or refrigerator.
11. To dip peels in chocolate, melt semisweet chocolate with butter over simmering water, stirring well to ensure a smooth mixture before removing from heat.
12. Dip each rind into chocolate, coating most of it while leaving a small "handle" to grasp, or completely immerse the rind and remove using two forks or chopsticks.
13. Place slices on parchment paper or a cake rack in a cool place to harden. Once set, store pieces in the refrigerator. Chocolate will discolor when cooled but will become dark and glossy again at room temperature.
Makes 3 Cups
DEBORAH MADISON - PRODIGY GUEST CHEFS COOKBOOK
